cases.title: FormatString maxLength — bounds the upper length
cases.description: stringFormat with an inclusive upper-length bound; rejects strings longer than `maxLength`
notes: Length 5 passes (`hello`); 6 chars (`hello!`) fails with `val` 5 (`maxLength`). A non-string (42) fails the string typeof gate before any format check. Empty string passes.

cases.title: FormatString minLength — bounds the lower length
cases.description: stringFormat with an inclusive lower-length bound; rejects strings shorter than `minLength`
notes: Length 3 passes (`abc`); 2 chars (`ab`) and the empty string both fail with `val` 3 (`minLength`).

cases.title: FormatString length — exact length only
cases.description: stringFormat requiring an exact length; rejects anything not exactly `length` chars
notes: Only length 4 passes (`abcd`); both 3 chars (`abc`) and 5 chars (`abcde`) fail with `val` 4 (`length`).

cases.title: FormatString minLength + maxLength — bounds both ends
cases.description: stringFormat with both inclusive length bounds; accepts lengths in `[minLength, maxLength]`
notes: Boundary lengths 2 (`ab`) and 4 (`abcd`) pass (inclusive). 1 char (`a`) fails with `val` 2 (`minLength`); 5 chars (`abcde`) fails with `val` 4 (`maxLength`).

cases.title: FormatString allowedChars — only the allowed set passes
cases.description: stringFormat restricting every char to the `allowedChars` set (hex digits); rejects any out-of-set char
notes: Each character must be in `0123456789abcdef`; `deadbeef` and `0042` pass.
`xyz` fails with `val` `Invalid characters`.
The space in `dead beef` is not in the set, so it also fails. The empty string passes (no chars to check).

cases.title: FormatString allowedChars ignoreCase — folds case
cases.description: stringFormat allowedChars with `ignoreCase`; both cases of the `abc` set are accepted
notes: Case-folded: `ABC` and `aAbBcC` pass even though only lowercase `abc` was listed. `abcd` fails with `val` `Invalid characters` (`d` not in the set).

cases.title: FormatString allowedChars — regex-special chars treated literally
cases.description: stringFormat allowedChars where regex-special chars are matched literally; only `.` and `-` pass
notes: The set `.-` is treated as literal chars (NOT a regex range), so `...---` passes. `a` fails with `val` `Invalid characters`.

cases.title: FormatString disallowedChars — rejects any disallowed char
cases.description: stringFormat blacklisting the `disallowedChars` set (`!@#`); any occurrence rejects the string
notes: A string passes only if it contains none of `!`, `@`, `#`; `hello` passes. `hi!` and `a@b` each fail with `val` `Invalid characters`.

cases.title: FormatString allowedValues — enum-like exact match
cases.description: stringFormat restricting the whole value to a fixed set (`red`/`green`/`blue`); enum-like exact match
notes: The entire string must equal one listed value; `red` and `blue` pass.
`yellow` (not listed) fails with `val` `Invalid value`.
Match is case-sensitive (`RED` fails) and whole-string (`redgreen` fails — no substring/concat).

cases.title: FormatString allowedValues ignoreCase — folds case across the set
cases.description: stringFormat allowedValues with `ignoreCase`; the fixed set matches regardless of case
notes: Case-folded equality: `RED` and `Green` pass. `blue` (not in the `red`/`green` set) fails with `val` `Invalid value`.

cases.title: FormatString allowedValues — regex-special chars matched literally
cases.description: stringFormat allowedValues where regex-special chars in the set are matched literally
notes: Listed values `a.b` and `c+d` match literally (the `.` and `+` are not regex metacharacters), so they pass. `axb` and `ccd` each fail with `val` `Invalid value`.

cases.title: FormatString disallowedValues — rejects the listed values
cases.description: stringFormat blacklisting whole values (`admin`/`root`); any other string passes
notes: A string passes unless it exactly equals a blacklisted value; `alice` passes. `admin` and `root` each fail with `val` `Invalid value`.

cases.title: FormatString allowedValues — custom errorMessage surfaces as format.val
cases.description: stringFormat allowedValues with a custom `errorMessage`; on failure the message surfaces as the format error `val`
notes: `a` and `b` pass. `c` fails with `val` `pick a or b` — the custom `errorMessage` replaces the default `Invalid value`.

cases.title: FormatAlpha — letters only
cases.description: FormatAlpha (stringFormat with a baked letters-only pattern); rejects digits, spaces, and symbols
notes: Only ASCII letters pass; `Hello` and `abcXYZ` pass.
A digit (`hello1`) or space (`hi there`) fails with `val` `Invalid pattern`.
The empty string passes (the pattern allows zero letters).

cases.title: FormatAlphaNumeric — letters and digits
cases.description: FormatAlphaNumeric (stringFormat with a baked letters+digits pattern); rejects everything else
notes: Letters and digits pass (`abc123`, `ABC`, `123`); a hyphen (`a-b`) or space (`a b`) fails with `val` `Invalid pattern`.

cases.title: FormatNumeric — digits only
cases.description: FormatNumeric (stringFormat with a baked digits-only pattern); rejects non-digit chars
notes: Only digit chars pass (`12345`, `007` — leading zeros allowed since it is a string). A decimal point (`12.3`) or letter (`12a`) fails with `val` `Invalid pattern`.

cases.title: FormatAlpha with maxLength — char class plus length bound
cases.description: FormatAlpha carrying a `maxLength` param; enforces letters-only AND an inclusive upper-length bound
notes: `abc` (3 letters) passes. `abcd` exceeds the bound and fails with `val` 3 (`maxLength`); `a1` is within length but the digit fails the pattern with `val` `Invalid pattern`.

cases.title: FormatLowercase — transformer-only, validates as a plain string
cases.description: FormatLowercase (transformer-only `lowercase` flag); validate treats it as a plain string
notes: The lowercase transform applies only via createFormatTransform, NOT validate — so ANY string passes regardless of case (`already lower` AND `HasUpper` pass). Only a non-string (42) fails, via the typeof gate.

cases.title: FormatUUIDv4 — accepts v4, rejects v7 and malformed
cases.description: FormatUUIDv4 (format `uuid`, version `4`); accepts only version-4 UUIDs
notes: Only a well-formed v4 UUID passes; the version nibble must be `4`.
A v7 UUID fails with `val` `4`; a non-UUID string (`not-a-uuid`) also fails with `val` `4`.
The empty string, a hyphen-stripped UUID, and a non-string (123) are all rejected.

cases.title: FormatUUIDv7 — accepts v7, rejects v4
cases.description: FormatUUIDv7 (format `uuid`, version `7`); accepts only version-7 UUIDs
notes: The version nibble must be `7`; a valid v4 UUID fails with `val` `7`.

cases.title: FormatStringDate — ISO / YYYY-MM-DD (default)
cases.description: FormatStringDate (format `date`) with the default ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` layout; enforces calendar validity
notes: Default layout is ISO `YYYY-MM-DD`; the format error `val` is `ISO`.
Calendar validity is enforced: `2023-02-29` (not a leap year), `2024-13-01` (month 13), and `2024-04-31` (April has 30 days) all fail.
Width is exact — `2024-1-1` (single-digit month/day) fails; `not-a-date` fails. `0001-01-01` is accepted.

cases.title: FormatStringDate — DD-MM-YYYY layout
cases.description: FormatStringDate with the `DD-MM-YYYY` layout; day-first ordering plus calendar validity
notes: Layout is `DD-MM-YYYY` (format error `val` `DD-MM-YYYY`); `29-02-2024` passes. An ISO-ordered string (`2024-02-29`) fails the layout, and `31-04-2024` fails calendar validity (April has 30 days).

cases.title: FormatStringDate — YYYY-MM layout (no day)
cases.description: FormatStringDate with the `YYYY-MM` layout (year-month, no day component)
notes: Layout is `YYYY-MM` (format error `val` `YYYY-MM`); `2024-02` passes. Month 13 (`2024-13`) fails, and supplying a day (`2024-02-29`) fails the layout.

cases.title: FormatStringDate — MM-DD layout (no year)
cases.description: FormatStringDate with the `MM-DD` layout (month-day, no year component)
notes: Layout is `MM-DD` (format error `val` `MM-DD`); `02-29` passes. Month 13 (`13-01`) fails.

cases.title: FormatStringDate — absolute min/max bounds (inclusive)
cases.description: FormatStringDate with inclusive absolute `min`/`max` date bounds; accepts dates within [`min`, `max`]
notes: Bounds `2020-01-01`..`2020-12-31` are inclusive — both endpoints pass. `2019-12-31` fails on `min` (formatPathTail `min`); `2021-01-01` fails on `max` (formatPathTail `max`).

cases.title: FormatStringTime — ISO (default, tz-aware)
cases.description: FormatStringTime (format `time`) with the default ISO layout; requires a timezone and valid time fields
notes: Default ISO layout (format error `val` `ISO`) requires a tz suffix; `12:30:45Z`, `12:30:45.123Z` (ms), and offset forms like `+05:30` / `-08:00` pass.
A tz-less time (`12:30:45`) fails. Field ranges are enforced: hour 24 (`24:00:00Z`) and minute 60 (`12:60:00Z`) both fail.

cases.title: FormatStringTime — HH:mm:ss fixed layout
cases.description: FormatStringTime with the fixed `HH:mm:ss` layout (no tz, no milliseconds)
notes: `23:59:59` passes. Out-of-range fields (`99:99:99`) fail with `val` `HH:mm:ss`; a missing seconds component (`23:59`) and hour 24 (`24:00:00`) are also rejected.

cases.title: FormatStringTime — HH:mm:ss[.mmm] optional milliseconds
cases.description: FormatStringTime with the `HH:mm:ss[.mmm]` layout; milliseconds optional and capped at 3 digits
notes: Milliseconds are optional — both `12:30:45` and `12:30:45.999` pass. A 4-digit fraction (`12:30:45.9999`) exceeds the `.mmm` width and fails with `val` `HH:mm:ss[.mmm]`.

cases.title: FormatStringTime — absolute min/max bounds (business hours)
cases.description: FormatStringTime with inclusive absolute `min`/`max` time bounds (HH:mm); accepts times within [`min`, `max`]
notes: Bounds `09:00`..`17:00` are inclusive — both endpoints pass. `08:59` fails on `min` (formatPathTail `min`); `17:01` fails on `max` (formatPathTail `max`).

cases.title: FormatStringDateTime — default (ISO date T ISO time)
cases.description: FormatStringDateTime (format `dateTime`) with the default ISO layout: ISO date, `T` split char, ISO tz-aware time
notes: Both halves must be individually valid and joined by `T`; `2024-02-29T12:30:45Z` passes.
A space separator (`2024-02-29 12:30:45Z`) fails on the split char (formatPathTail `splitChar`).
A non-leap date (`2023-02-29`), an out-of-range hour (`25:30:45`), and `not-a-datetime` are all rejected.

cases.title: FormatStringDateTime — custom nested layouts + splitChar
cases.description: FormatStringDateTime with custom nested `date`/`time` layouts and a space `splitChar`; each part validated independently
notes: Layout is `DD-MM-YYYY` date + `HH:mm` time joined by a space; `29-02-2024 23:59` passes.
An ISO-ordered date (`2024-02-29 23:59`) fails on the date half (formatPathTail `date`).
A `T` separator (`29-02-2024T23:59`) fails the split char (formatPathTail `splitChar`); hour 24 (`29-02-2024 24:00`) fails the time half (formatPathTail `time`).

cases.title: FormatStringDateTime — absolute min/max bounds
cases.description: FormatStringDateTime with inclusive absolute `min`/`max` datetime bounds; accepts values within [`min`, `max`]
notes: Bounds `2020-01-01T00:00:00`..`2020-12-31T23:59:59` are inclusive — both endpoints pass. `2019-12-31T23:59:59` fails on `min` (formatPathTail `min`); `2021-01-01T00:00:00` fails on `max` (formatPathTail `max`).

cases.title: FormatIPv4 — dotted-quad addresses
cases.description: FormatIPv4 (format `ip`, version 4); accepts dotted-quad IPv4 addresses only
notes: Out-of-range octets (`999.999.999.999`, `256.0.0.1`), a 3-octet address (`1.2.3`), and an IPv6 address (`::1`) all fail; the first failure carries `val` 4.

cases.title: FormatIPv6 — colon-separated, loopback allowed
cases.description: FormatIPv6 (format `ip`, version 6); accepts colon-separated IPv6 addresses (including `::` compression)
notes: Full, compressed (`::1`), and link-local (`fe80::1`) forms pass. An IPv4 address (`192.168.0.1`) and a group exceeding 4 hex digits (`12345::1`) each fail with `val` 6.

cases.title: FormatIP — accepts both v4 and v6
cases.description: FormatIP (format `ip`, version `any`); accepts either an IPv4 or an IPv6 address
notes: Both `10.0.0.1` (v4) and `2001:db8::1` (v6) pass. A non-IP string (`definitely not an ip`) fails with `val` `any`.

cases.title: FormatIPv4WithPort — v4 with port
cases.description: FormatIPv4WithPort (format `ip`, version 4, port allowed); accepts `ipv4:port`
notes: The port must be in range; `192.168.0.1:8080` passes, while `192.168.0.1:70000` (port > 65535) fails with `val` 4.

cases.title: FormatIPv6WithPort — v6 with bracketed port
cases.description: FormatIPv6WithPort (format `ip`, version 6, port allowed); accepts bracketed `[ipv6]:port`
notes: The port must be in range; `[2001:db8::1]:443` passes, while `[2001:db8::1]:99999` (port > 65535) fails with `val` 6.

cases.title: FormatDomain — standard
cases.description: FormatDomain (format `domain`); enforces the baked domain pattern plus `minLength` 5 / `maxLength` 253
notes: Multi-label hostnames pass (`mion.io`, `example.com`, `sub.example.co.uk`, `a-b.example.org`).

Rejected: a bare label (`not-a-domain`), a leading dot (`.com`), a single-char TLD (`example.c`), a leading-hyphen label (`-bad.com`), an embedded space (`exa mple.com`), and the empty string. The format error is `{name: domain}` (no `val`).

cases.title: FormatDomainStrict — names/tld decomposition, maxParts, hyphen-edge
cases.description: FormatDomainStrict (format `domain`); stricter than FormatDomain — ≤6 labels, ≥2 parts, strict name/TLD patterns
notes: Up to 6 labels pass (`mion.io`, `sub.example.com`, `aa.bb.cc.dd.ee.com`).

Rejected: a leading-hyphen label (`-bad.com`), more than 6 labels (`aa.bb.cc.dd.ee.ff.com`), a numeric TLD (`example.123`), an underscore in a label (`ex_ample.com`), and a single-part name (`localhost`). The format error is `{name: domain}` (no `val`).

cases.title: FormatEmail — standard
cases.description: FormatEmail (format `email`); enforces the baked email pattern plus `minLength` 7 / `maxLength` 254
notes: Standard addresses pass, including subaddressing (`user+tag@sub.example.org`).

Rejected: no `@` (`not-an-email`), too short (`a@b.co`, below `minLength` 7), missing local part (`@example.com`), missing domain (`john@`), a TLD-less domain (`john@example`), an embedded space (`john doe@example.com`), and the empty string. The format error is `{name: email}` (no `val`).

cases.title: FormatEmailPunycode — accepts punycode-tld domains
cases.description: FormatEmailPunycode (format `email`); email pattern that additionally accepts punycode (`xn--`) domain labels
notes: A punycode-TLD address (`john@example.xn--fiqs8s`) passes. A non-email string (`not-an-email`) fails with `{name: email}` (no `val`).

cases.title: FormatEmailStrict — localPart + domain decomposition
cases.description: FormatEmailStrict (format `email`); splits on the last `@`, then applies a strict local-part pattern + strict domain
notes: Plain addresses pass (`john@example.com`, `jane.doe@mion.io`).
A disallowed local-part char (`a+b@x.com`) fails with `val` `Invalid characters in email local part`.
Also rejected: a space in the local part (`a b@example.com`), a doubled `@` (`john@@example.com`), an underscore in the domain (`john@bad_domain.com`), and no `@` at all (`no-at-symbol`).

cases.title: FormatUrl — standard (http/ftp/ws schemes)
cases.description: FormatUrl (format `url`, `maxLength` 2048); accepts common schemes (http, ftp, ws/wss)
notes: Multiple schemes pass (`https://`, `http://` with path+query, `ftp://`, `wss://`).
Rejected: a scheme-less string (`not-a-url`), a bare host (`example.com`), a `mailto:` URI, and a scheme with no host (`https://`). The format error is `{name: url}` (no `val`).

cases.title: FormatUrlHttp — http(s) only
cases.description: FormatUrlHttp (format `url`); restricts the scheme to `http` / `https`
notes: Both `https://example.com` and `http://example.com` pass; a non-http scheme (`ftp://example.com`) fails with `{name: url}` (no `val`).

cases.title: FormatUrlFile — file URLs
cases.description: FormatUrlFile (format `url`); restricts the scheme to `file:`
notes: A `file:///etc/hosts` URL passes; a non-file scheme (`https://example.com`) fails with `{name: url}` (no `val`).

cases.title: registerFormatPattern — slug regex recovered from the call site
cases.description: stringFormat with a registered `pattern` (slug `^[a-z0-9-]+$`); only lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens pass
notes: Lowercase slug strings pass (`my-slug`, `a-b-c`).
Rejected: capitals (`Has Capitals`, `UPPER`), an embedded space (`has space`), and the empty string.
Although the pattern registers a custom message (`must be a slug`), validate surfaces the static default `val` `Invalid pattern` (the `message` field is excluded from cache identity).

cases.title: registerFormatPattern — {source, flags} overload (case-insensitive)
cases.description: stringFormat with a registered case-insensitive `pattern` (hex `^[0-9a-f]+$`, flag `i`); accepts hex digits in either case
notes: The `i` flag folds case, so both `0042` and `DEADbeef` pass. A non-hex string (`xyz`) and the empty string each fail with `val` `Invalid pattern`.

cases.title: FormatNumber<{max: 100}> — inclusive upper bound
cases.description: numberFormat with an inclusive upper bound; rejects numbers above max
notes: Boundary value 100 passes (inclusive); 101 fails on `max`. A non-number ("5") fails the number typeof gate before any format check.

cases.title: FormatNumber<{min: 0}> — inclusive lower bound
cases.description: numberFormat with an inclusive lower bound; rejects numbers below min (equivalent to FormatPositive)
notes: Boundary value 0 passes (inclusive); -1 fails on `min`.

cases.title: FormatNumber<{lt: 10}> — exclusive upper bound
cases.description: numberFormat with an exclusive upper bound; the bound itself is rejected
notes: Exclusive `lt`: 9 passes but the boundary 10 fails (and 11 above it). Lower bound is unconstrained, so -100 passes.

cases.title: FormatNumber<{gt: 0}> — exclusive lower bound
cases.description: numberFormat with an exclusive lower bound; the bound itself is rejected
notes: Exclusive `gt`: 1 passes but the boundary 0 fails (and -1 below it).

cases.title: FormatInteger — whole numbers only
cases.description: numberFormat with the `integer` flag; rejects any non-whole number
notes: Whole numbers (incl. 0 and negatives like -1) pass; fractional values (1.5, 3.14) fail on `integer`. No min/max bound, so any integer magnitude is accepted.

cases.title: FormatFloat — non-integer only
cases.description: numberFormat with the `float` flag; rejects whole numbers (the inverse of FormatInteger)
notes: Fractional values (1.5, -0.5, 3.14) pass; whole numbers (1, 0, -2) fail on `float`. `float` and `integer` are mutually exclusive.

cases.title: FormatNumber<{multipleOf: 5}> — divisible by 5
cases.description: numberFormat divisibility constraint; only multiples of 5 pass
notes: 0 counts as a multiple and passes; non-multiples (3, 7) fail on `multipleOf`. Negative multiples like -15 pass.

cases.title: FormatNumber<{min:0; max:100; integer:true; multipleOf:5}> — all constraints
cases.description: numberFormat combining min, max, integer, and multipleOf; each invalid sample trips a distinct constraint
notes: All four constraints enforced together: -5 fails `min`, 105 fails `max`, 7 fails `multipleOf`, 2.5 fails `integer`. Boundary values 0 and 100 pass (both inclusive, integers, and multiples of 5).

cases.title: FormatInt8 — signed 8-bit range
cases.description: numberFormat preset for the signed 8-bit range [-128, 127], integer-only; selects 1-byte binary packing
notes: Inclusive bounds min -128 / max 127, integer required: 128 fails `max`, -129 fails `min`, 1.5 fails `integer`. The fixed min/max drive the 1-byte binary packing optimization.

cases.title: FormatUInt8 — unsigned 8-bit range
cases.description: numberFormat preset for the unsigned 8-bit range [0, 255], integer-only; selects 1-byte binary packing
notes: Inclusive bounds min 0 / max 255, integer required: 256 fails `max`, -1 fails `min`. The fixed min/max drive the 1-byte binary packing optimization.

cases.title: FormatBigInt<{max: 100n}> — inclusive upper bound
cases.description: bigintFormat with an inclusive upper bound; rejects bigints above max
notes: Boundary value 100n passes (inclusive); 101n fails on `max`. A non-bigint (5) fails the bigint typeof gate before any format check.

cases.title: FormatBigInt<{min: 0n}> — inclusive lower bound
cases.description: bigintFormat with an inclusive lower bound; rejects bigints below min
notes: Boundary value 0n passes (inclusive); -1n fails on `min`.

cases.title: FormatBigInt<{lt: 10n}> — exclusive upper bound
cases.description: bigintFormat with an exclusive upper bound; the bound itself is rejected
notes: Exclusive `lt`: 9n passes but the boundary 10n fails (and 11n above it). Lower bound is unconstrained, so -5n passes.

cases.title: FormatBigInt<{gt: 0n}> — exclusive lower bound
cases.description: bigintFormat with an exclusive lower bound; the bound itself is rejected
notes: Exclusive `gt`: 1n passes but the boundary 0n fails (and -1n below it).

cases.title: FormatBigInt<{multipleOf: 5n}> — divisible by 5
cases.description: bigintFormat divisibility constraint; only multiples of 5n pass
notes: 0n counts as a multiple and passes; non-multiples (3n, 7n) fail on `multipleOf`. Negative multiples like -15n pass.

cases.title: FormatBigInt<{min:0n; max:1000n; multipleOf:10n}> — all constraints
cases.description: bigintFormat combining min, max, and multipleOf; each invalid sample trips a distinct constraint
notes: All three bounds enforced together: -10n fails `min`, 1010n fails `max`, 7n fails `multipleOf`. Boundary values 0n and 1000n pass (both inclusive and multiples of 10n).

cases.title: FormatBigInt64 — full signed 64-bit range
cases.description: bigintFormat preset for the signed 64-bit range [-2^63, 2^63-1]; selects 8-byte binary packing
notes: Inclusive bounds min -9223372036854775808n / max 9223372036854775807n; one past either end (2^63 / -(2^63)-1) fails on `max` / `min` respectively.

cases.title: FormatBigUInt64 — full unsigned 64-bit range
cases.description: bigintFormat preset for the unsigned 64-bit range [0, 2^64-1]; selects 8-byte binary packing
notes: Inclusive bounds min 0n / max 18446744073709551615n; 2^64 fails `max` and -1n fails `min`.

cases.title: FormatDate<{min,max}> — inclusive edges pass, one step outside fails
cases.description: Native `Date` with an inclusive `min`/`max` window; rejects dates outside [min, max]
notes: Inclusive bounds: both 2020-01-01T00:00:00 (`min`) and 2020-12-31T23:59:59 (`max`) pass as the exact boundary values.
One step outside fails: 2019-12-31T23:59:59 trips `min`, 2021-01-01T00:00:00 trips `max`; a non-Date value (`not-a-date`) is also rejected.

cases.title: FormatDate<{gt,lt}> — exclusive edges rejected, interior passes
cases.description: Native `Date` with exclusive `gt`/`lt` bounds; only strictly-interior dates pass
notes: Exclusive bounds: an interior date (2020-06-15) passes, but the boundary values themselves fail — 2020-01-01T00:00:00 trips `gt` and 2020-12-31T23:59:59 trips `lt`.
A non-Date value (`not-a-date`) is also rejected.

cases.title: FormatDate<{min,lt}> — inclusive lower + exclusive upper
cases.description: Native `Date` mixing an inclusive lower `min` with an exclusive upper `lt`
notes: Mixed edges: the lower bound 2020-01-01T00:00:00 (`min`, inclusive) passes, but the upper bound 2020-12-31T23:59:59 (`lt`, exclusive) fails.
Below-range 2019-12-31T23:59:59 trips `min`; an interior date (2020-06-15) passes.

cases.title: FormatDate<{max: now}> — rejects the future (relative)
cases.description: Native `Date` with an inclusive relative upper bound `max: now`; rejects future dates
notes: The `max` bound is the relative anchor `now`, resolved at validation time — a past date (2020-01-01) passes, a far-future date (2999-01-01) trips `max`.
Lower bound is unconstrained; a non-Date value (`not-a-date`) is also rejected.

cases.title: FormatDate<{min: now-P1000Y, max: now+P1000Y}> — relative window (Y, both components allowed)
cases.description: Native `Date` with a relative inclusive window `now-P1000Y`/`now+P1000Y` (year components, both allowed for Date)
notes: Both bounds are relative durations anchored at validation time (`now` ± 1000 years); a present-day date (2020-06-15) passes.
Far outside the wide window fails: year 1000 trips `min`, year 3500 trips `max`. The margin is deliberately huge so the boolean result holds regardless of the wall clock.

cases.title: FormatDate<{min: now-P1000YT12H}> — relative with both date + time components
cases.description: Native `Date` with a relative inclusive lower bound only (`now-P1000YT12H`, mixing year + hour components)
notes: Single relative `min` anchored at validation time (1000 years and 12 hours ago); the upper bound is open.
A present-day date (2020-06-15) passes; far-past year 1000 trips `min`. Date accepts both date (Y) and time (T) duration components.

cases.title: FormatTemporalInstant<{min,max}> — inclusive edges
cases.description: `Temporal.Instant` with an inclusive `min`/`max` window; rejects instants outside [min, max]
notes: Inclusive bounds: both the `min` instant (2020-01-01T00:00:00Z) and the `max` instant (2020-12-31T23:59:59Z) pass as exact boundaries.
One second outside fails: 2019-12-31T23:59:59Z trips `min`, 2021-01-01T00:00:00Z trips `max`; a non-Instant value is also rejected.

cases.title: FormatTemporalInstant<{gt,lt}> — exclusive edges rejected
cases.description: `Temporal.Instant` with exclusive `gt`/`lt` bounds; only strictly-interior instants pass
notes: Exclusive bounds: an interior instant (2020-06-15T12:00:00Z) passes, but the boundary instants themselves fail — 2020-01-01T00:00:00Z trips `gt`, 2020-12-31T23:59:59Z trips `lt`.
A non-Instant value is also rejected.

cases.title: FormatTemporalInstant<{min: now-PT8760000H, max: now+PT8760000H}> — relative (time components only)
cases.description: `Temporal.Instant` with a relative inclusive window expressed in hours (`now±PT8760000H`); Instant only accepts time components
notes: Both bounds are relative durations anchored at validation time (±8,760,000 hours ≈ ±1000 years); a present-day instant (2020-06-15T12:00:00Z) passes.
Far outside the wide window fails: year 1000 trips `min`, year 3500 trips `max`. The margin is huge so the result holds regardless of the wall clock.

cases.title: FormatTemporalPlainDate<{min,max}> — inclusive edges
cases.description: `Temporal.PlainDate` with an inclusive `min`/`max` window; rejects dates outside [min, max]
notes: Inclusive bounds: both 2020-01-01 (`min`) and 2020-12-31 (`max`) pass as exact boundaries.
One day outside fails: 2019-12-31 trips `min`, 2021-01-01 trips `max`; a wrong-type value (a `Temporal.Instant`) is also rejected.

cases.title: FormatTemporalPlainDate<{gt,lt}> — exclusive edges rejected, next day inside passes
cases.description: `Temporal.PlainDate` with exclusive `gt`/`lt` bounds; only strictly-interior dates pass
notes: Exclusive bounds: the next day inside each edge passes (2020-01-02, 2020-12-30), but the boundary dates themselves fail — 2020-01-01 trips `gt`, 2020-12-31 trips `lt`.
A non-date value is also rejected.

cases.title: FormatTemporalPlainDate<{min,lt}> — inclusive lower + exclusive upper
cases.description: `Temporal.PlainDate` mixing an inclusive lower `min` with an exclusive upper `lt`
notes: Mixed edges: the lower bound 2020-01-01 (`min`, inclusive) passes, but the upper bound 2020-01-10 (`lt`, exclusive) fails; the day before it (2020-01-09) passes.
Below-range 2019-12-31 trips `min`.

cases.title: FormatTemporalPlainDate<{gt,max}> — exclusive lower + inclusive upper
cases.description: `Temporal.PlainDate` mixing an exclusive lower `gt` with an inclusive upper `max`
notes: Mixed edges: the lower bound 2020-01-01 (`gt`, exclusive) fails while the day after (2020-01-02) passes; the upper bound 2020-01-10 (`max`, inclusive) passes.
Above-range 2020-01-11 trips `max`.

cases.title: FormatTemporalPlainDate<{min}> — lower bound only
cases.description: `Temporal.PlainDate` with an inclusive lower `min` only; upper end is open
notes: Inclusive `min`: the boundary 2020-01-01 passes, as does any later date (2099-12-31); only 2019-12-31 (below `min`) fails.

cases.title: FormatTemporalPlainDate<{max}> — upper bound only
cases.description: `Temporal.PlainDate` with an inclusive upper `max` only; lower end is open
notes: Inclusive `max`: the boundary 2020-12-31 passes, as does any earlier date (1900-01-01); only 2021-01-01 (above `max`) fails.

cases.title: FormatTemporalPlainDate<{gt}> — exclusive lower bound only
cases.description: `Temporal.PlainDate` with an exclusive lower `gt` only; upper end is open
notes: Exclusive `gt`: the next day inside (2020-01-02) passes, but the boundary 2020-01-01 fails and so does the earlier 2019-12-31 — both invalid samples trip `gt`.

cases.title: FormatTemporalPlainDate<{lt}> — exclusive upper bound only
cases.description: `Temporal.PlainDate` with an exclusive upper `lt` only; lower end is open
notes: Exclusive `lt`: the day before (2020-12-30) passes, but the boundary 2020-12-31 fails and so does the later 2021-06-01 — both invalid samples trip `lt`.

cases.title: FormatTemporalPlainDate<{min: now-P1000Y, max: now+P1000Y}> — relative window (Y)
cases.description: `Temporal.PlainDate` with a relative inclusive window `now-P1000Y`/`now+P1000Y` (year components)
notes: Both bounds are relative durations anchored at validation time (`now` ± 1000 years); a present-day date (2020-06-15) passes.
Far outside the wide window fails: year 0500 trips `min`, year 3500 trips `max`. The margin is huge so the result holds regardless of the wall clock.

cases.title: FormatTemporalPlainDate<{min: now-P100Y6M15D}> — relative Y/M/D components
cases.description: `Temporal.PlainDate` with a relative inclusive lower bound only (`now-P100Y6M15D`, year/month/day components); upper end is open
notes: Single relative `min` anchored at validation time (100 years, 6 months, 15 days ago); a present-day date (2020-06-15) passes while far-past 1800-01-01 trips `min`.

cases.title: FormatTemporalPlainDate<{min: now-P52200W}> — relative W component
cases.description: `Temporal.PlainDate` with a relative inclusive lower bound only expressed in weeks (`now-P52200W` ≈ 1000 years); upper end is open
notes: Single relative `min` anchored at validation time (52,200 weeks ≈ 1000 years ago); a present-day date (2020-06-15) passes while far-past year 0500 trips `min`. The week (W) component is valid for date kinds.

cases.title: FormatTemporalPlainTime<{min,max}> — inclusive edges (business hours)
cases.description: `Temporal.PlainTime` with an inclusive `min`/`max` window (business hours 09:00–17:00); rejects times outside [min, max]
notes: Inclusive bounds: both 09:00:00 (`min`) and 17:00:00 (`max`) pass as exact boundaries.
One second outside fails: 08:59:59 trips `min`, 17:00:01 trips `max`; a non-time value is also rejected.

cases.title: FormatTemporalPlainTime<{gt,lt}> — exclusive edges rejected
cases.description: `Temporal.PlainTime` with exclusive `gt`/`lt` bounds; only strictly-interior times pass
notes: Exclusive bounds: one second inside each edge passes (09:00:01, 16:59:59), but the boundary times themselves fail — 09:00:00 trips `gt`, 17:00:00 trips `lt`.
A non-time value is also rejected.

cases.title: FormatTemporalPlainDateTime<{min,max}> — inclusive edges
cases.description: `Temporal.PlainDateTime` with an inclusive `min`/`max` window; rejects datetimes outside [min, max]
notes: Inclusive bounds: both 2020-01-01T00:00:00 (`min`) and 2020-12-31T23:59:59 (`max`) pass as exact boundaries.
One second outside fails: 2019-12-31T23:59:59 trips `min`, 2021-01-01T00:00:00 trips `max`; a non-datetime value is also rejected.

cases.title: FormatTemporalPlainDateTime<{gt,lt}> — exclusive edges rejected
cases.description: `Temporal.PlainDateTime` with exclusive `gt`/`lt` bounds; only strictly-interior datetimes pass
notes: Exclusive bounds: an interior datetime (2020-06-15T12:00:00) passes, but the boundary datetimes themselves fail — 2020-01-01T00:00:00 trips `gt`, 2020-12-31T23:59:59 trips `lt`.
A non-datetime value is also rejected.

cases.title: FormatTemporalPlainDateTime<{min: now-P1000Y, max: now+P1000Y}> — relative window
cases.description: `Temporal.PlainDateTime` with a relative inclusive window `now-P1000Y`/`now+P1000Y` (year components)
notes: Both bounds are relative durations anchored at validation time (`now` ± 1000 years); a present-day datetime (2020-06-15T12:00:00) passes.
Far outside the wide window fails: year 0500 trips `min`, year 3500 trips `max`. The margin is huge so the result holds regardless of the wall clock.

cases.title: FormatTemporalPlainDateTime<{min: now-P500YT12H}> — relative date + time components
cases.description: `Temporal.PlainDateTime` with a relative inclusive lower bound only (`now-P500YT12H`, mixing year + hour components); upper end is open
notes: Single relative `min` anchored at validation time (500 years and 12 hours ago); a present-day datetime (2020-06-15T12:00:00) passes while far-past year 1000 trips `min`. DateTime kinds accept both date (Y) and time (T) duration components.

cases.title: FormatTemporalPlainYearMonth<{min,max}> — inclusive edges
cases.description: `Temporal.PlainYearMonth` with an inclusive `min`/`max` window; rejects year-months outside [min, max]
notes: Inclusive bounds: both 2020-01 (`min`) and 2020-12 (`max`) pass as exact boundaries.
One month outside fails: 2019-12 trips `min`, 2021-01 trips `max`; a non-year-month value is also rejected.

cases.title: FormatTemporalPlainYearMonth<{gt,lt}> — exclusive edges rejected
cases.description: `Temporal.PlainYearMonth` with exclusive `gt`/`lt` bounds; only strictly-interior year-months pass
notes: Exclusive bounds: the next month inside each edge passes (2020-02, 2020-11), but the boundary year-months themselves fail — 2020-01 trips `gt`, 2020-12 trips `lt`.
A non-year-month value is also rejected.

cases.title: FormatTemporalPlainYearMonth<{min: now-P1000Y, max: now+P1000Y}> — relative (Y/M)
cases.description: `Temporal.PlainYearMonth` with a relative inclusive window `now-P1000Y`/`now+P1000Y` (year/month components)
notes: Both bounds are relative durations anchored at validation time (`now` ± 1000 years); a present-day year-month (2020-06) passes.
Far outside the wide window fails: 0500-01 trips `min`, 3500-01 trips `max`. The margin is huge so the result holds regardless of the wall clock.

cases.title: FormatTemporalZonedDateTime<{min,max}> — inclusive edges
cases.description: `Temporal.ZonedDateTime` (UTC) with an inclusive `min`/`max` window; rejects instants outside [min, max]
notes: Inclusive bounds: both 2020-01-01T00:00:00[UTC] (`min`) and 2020-12-31T23:59:59[UTC] (`max`) pass as exact boundaries.
One second outside fails: 2019-12-31T23:59:59[UTC] trips `min`, 2021-01-01T00:00:00[UTC] trips `max`; a non-zoned value is also rejected.

cases.title: FormatTemporalZonedDateTime<{gt,lt}> — exclusive edges rejected
cases.description: `Temporal.ZonedDateTime` (UTC) with exclusive `gt`/`lt` bounds; only strictly-interior instants pass
notes: Exclusive bounds: an interior datetime (2020-06-15T12:00:00[UTC]) passes, but the boundary values themselves fail — 2020-01-01T00:00:00[UTC] trips `gt`, 2020-12-31T23:59:59[UTC] trips `lt`.
A non-zoned value is also rejected.

cases.title: FormatTemporalZonedDateTime<{min: now-P1000Y, max: now+P1000Y}> — relative window
cases.description: `Temporal.ZonedDateTime` (UTC) with a relative inclusive window `now-P1000Y`/`now+P1000Y` (year components)
notes: Both bounds are relative durations anchored at validation time (`now` ± 1000 years); a present-day datetime (2020-06-15T12:00:00[UTC]) passes.
Far outside the wide window fails: year 0500 trips `min`, year 3500 trips `max`. The margin is huge so the result holds regardless of the wall clock.
